mnemonics: TRY to CATCH the Exception: Imagine trying to perform an action (TRY) and having a safety net (CATCH) to grab you if you fall (an exception occurs).
keyPoints: A try block encloses code that might throw an exception.
A catch block handles a specific type of exception thrown by the try block.
Multiple catch blocks can be used to handle different types of exceptions.
commonMistakes: Using overly broad catch blocks (e.g., catch(Exception e)) which can hide specific errors.
Placing code after the try-catch that depends on the try block's success without proper checks.
Forgetting that an exception in the try block immediately jumps to the catch, skipping remaining try code.
